description: EXPECTS TO EXPAND MORE RAPIDLY BY 71970 Computer Industry Has Grown About 377 Per Cent Since 1956 By MARTIN ARUNDEL NEW YORK NANA Since 1956 the uedging computer in dustry has grown about 377 per cent one of the fastest growths in US industrial history and it expects to expand far mnre rap idly between now and 1970 ac cording to the industrys latest reports The 18 leading US electronic data processing equipment man mfacturers and numerouws service companies some of the big manufacturers have their own service subsidiaries in the field did a combised estimated 4212 000000 business in 1962 exceed ing that of 1961 by 336 per cent and 1960s by 55 per cent The industry rang up a 882400000 volume in 1956 By 1960 the industry will be in the 20000000000ayear class making it one of the coun trys largest a recent report by the industrial securities commit tee of the Investment Bankers Association of America IBBA predicts Computers now rank 12th among US industries According to a recent count there are now 10699 electronic computers in operation in this country about 54 per cent more than a year ago says Busi ness Automation the industrys monthly
